concurrencyGaps: The single highest-priority gap: whatsappInbox.ts:757 Appointment.create has NO advisory-lock/overlap-safety test coverage at all — see F0004-E0684 above. This is the exact double-booking scenario the domain’s own safer pattern (appointmentRequestSafety.ts) already exists to prevent, just not on this path.

Inventory’s two independent cross-domain write paths (Treatment Cases’ routes/treatmentCases.ts and Dental Chart’s services/treatmentStockDeduction.ts both writing InventoryItem/InventoryTransaction) have no concurrency test proving they cannot double-deduct stock under simultaneous requests.
tenantSecurityGaps: multiBranchAccess.test.ts (142 test cases, the single largest test file by case count) is a PURE reimplementation — it deliberately does not import server/src/utils/clinicScope.ts, middleware/clinicAccess.ts, or any route/middleware code (documented in its own header: “import mock yok”). The actual production tenant-scoping code path has ZERO direct test coverage; only a hand-written parallel mirror of its intended logic is tested. The same drift risk demonstrated by overdueInstallments.test.ts (see §9 Failures) applies here at much larger scale if clinicScope.ts/clinicAccess.ts ever changes without someone remembering to update this 1043-line mirror.
treatmentCaseClinicScope.test.ts similarly reimplements clinic-scope logic inline rather than importing the real route file.
providerFailureGaps: WhatsApp/Instagram/SMS/Email provider FAILURE-path behavior (rate limiting, retries, partial outages) is tested only at the unit level via injected globalThis.fetch stubs returning canned error responses (whatsappProvider.test.ts, instagramProvider.test.ts, metaTemplateService.test.ts) — there is no test proving end-to-end behavior when a provider is genuinely unreachable in the full inbound/outbound pipeline.
migrationGaps: No automated migration up/down test exists in the repository. The 3 server/scripts/verify-*.ts scripts are the closest equivalent (real-Postgres lifecycle proofs for specific KVKK features) but are explicitly NOT wired into any test runner and require a manually-provisioned disposable database — see §8 Runtime Results.

securityIncident.test.ts, communicationConsent.test.ts, and communicationPreferenceBackfill.test.ts are the only 3 .test.ts files that require a real database connection, and none of the 3 could be executed to completion in this environment (see §8/§9 and the rebaseline note in §1a) — meaning the security-incident domain's and the Privacy domain's new consent/backfill DATABASE_INTEGRATION coverage are both entirely unverified in this baseline.
frontendGaps: The frontend has NO test runner, NO React Testing Library, and NO DOM/component-rendering test harness of any kind (confirmed by explicit statements in all 5 frontend test file headers). All 5 frontend tests are FRONTEND_UTILITY-only — pure extracted-function logic. There is zero FRONTEND_COMPONENT coverage anywhere in the repository.
Only 5 of the repository’s ~64 frontend pages/components (per F0-003’s inventory) have any test coverage at all.
bridgeGaps: The 4 windows-bridge .NET test projects (354 approx. Fact/Theory test methods total) could NOT be executed in this task — windows-bridge/global.json pins .NET SDK 10.0.301, and only 9.0.305 is installed on this machine. This is a BLOCKED measurement, not a coverage judgment — see §8/§9.

The 4 installer PowerShell test scripts (58 cases) DID run successfully under Windows PowerShell 5.1, despite CI declaring `shell: pwsh` (PowerShell 7) — this is a positive finding (the scripts are more portable than CI assumes) but also means CI’s exact interpreter has never been cross-verified against 5.1 in this task.
loadChaosGaps: No load or chaos test exists anywhere in the repository (confirmed absent — no k6/Artillery/Locust config, no chaos-engineering tooling, no fault-injection harness beyond the in-process fake-dependency-throws pattern used inside individual unit tests).
ciEnforcementGap: Exactly ONE GitHub Actions workflow exists that runs any tests at all: .github/workflows/windows-bridge-pr.yml, and it is scoped ONLY to paths touching windows-bridge/**, server/src/services/imaging/**, server/src/routes/imaging*.ts, server/src/tests/imaging*.ts, and src/components/imaging/**. It runs 4 of 70 backend test files (test:imaging, test:imaging-bridge-pairing/onboarding/update), 3 of 5 frontend test files (test:dicom-helpers/onboarding-helpers/pairing-poller — NOT test:booking-widget-helpers or test:clinic-bulk-export-selection), the 4 dotnet test projects, and the 4 installer PowerShell scripts.

NONE of the other 66 backend test files, NONE of the 9 bridge-agent test files, and 2 of the 5 frontend test files have ANY CI enforcement whatsoever. `npm run test` (the full 54-script backend chain) is never invoked by any workflow. This is the direct, repository-proven explanation for how overdueInstallments.test.ts’s genuine 2-assertion regression (see §9) went undetected: it is one of 6 backend .test.ts files reachable by NO package.json script at all, and even the 60 test files that DO have scripts are not run by CI unless they touch the imaging path.
fullSuiteOmissions: `npm run test` (54 chained scripts) omits 6 orphan scripts that exist in package.json but are never called from the chain: test:consent-resume, test:meta-template, test:outbound, test:no-show-follow-up-parity, test:overdue-installments, test:overdue-receivables.

6 backend .test.ts files have NO package.json script at all and can only be invoked via direct `npx tsx src/tests/<file>.test.ts`: aiPrivacyBoundary.test.ts, channelConsentGate.test.ts, clinicLegalProfile.test.ts, patientSharedPhone.test.ts, platformBackup.test.ts, treatmentPackagePermissions.test.ts.
